messages.content: why did capacity spike?

script.text: No live capacity signal is configured, so I can't confirm a spike — enable monitoring.

messages.content: why did capacity spike on 2026-06-08?

script.text: The capacity peaked at 96% with 42 minutes of throttling, driven by concurrent 06:00 refreshes in the Finance workspace.

messages.content: which specific operations caused the most recent spike?

script.text: The costliest individual operation was a QueryEnd on the Finance dataset consuming 30.0 CU-seconds, making it the single largest spike event in the estate.

messages.content: what ran between 12:45 and 13:00 today?

script.text: In that window, alice@co ran a query against Sales costing 12.0 CU-seconds.

messages.content: Give me the full capacity audit for our tenant.

script.text: The audit found 17 findings (9 critical, 7 warning); capacity peaked at 96% CU with a 42-minute throttle window, driven largely by the oversized 4.2GB Sales model in Finance.

messages.content: List all our Fabric workspaces and their activity.

script.text: I can't inventory the workspaces — no live telemetry source is configured; enable monitoring (Log Analytics or Workspace Monitoring) to see the real estate.

messages.content: Who are the top users by CU consumption?

script.text: I can't rank top users right now — the offline activity feed has zero attributed users; enable monitoring to get real coverage.

messages.content: What's driving alice@co's capacity usage?

script.text: Analysis of alice@co is insufficient — the evidence doesn't support a defensible conclusion; enable monitoring on additional workspaces to get real coverage.

messages.content: Show alice@co's spike history.

script.text: alice@co has 1 recorded spike totaling 20.0 CU-seconds, peaking at 12.0 CU-seconds against the Sales dataset.

messages.content: Are there any patterns coupling user surges with CU spikes today?

script.text: One pattern shows 5 active users driving an 85% CU peak in the Finance workspace, led by eve@co.

messages.content: What columns does the events telemetry source have before I query it?

script.text: The events source is table PowerBIDatasetsWorkspace with columns including TimeGenerated and ExecutingUser (mock schema, since no live source is configured).

messages.content: Give me a few sample raw events before I run a heavier query.

script.text: A sample of 5 raw events shows alice@co querying Sales for 8.0 CU-seconds and dave@co refreshing HR for 20.0 CU-seconds.

messages.content: Give me live capacity diagnostics — resource, consumed, remaining.

script.text: I can't provide capacity diagnostics — the Capacity Events cluster isn't configured; enable monitoring (set FABRIC_CAPACITY_EVENTS_CLUSTER) to get live diagnostics.

messages.content: Does this DAX measure have any anti-patterns: EVALUATE FILTER(Sales, Sales[Amount] > 0)?

script.text: Static analysis flags 1 anti-pattern: filter-whole-table — FILTER() over an entire table is slow; filter a single column instead.

messages.content: Why is the capacity throttling right now?

script.text: The throttle diagnostic eliminated capacity over-utilization as the cause: CU% peaked at 85.0%, with 0 timepoints crossing the throttling threshold.

messages.content: What's changed since the last scheduled sweep?

script.text: I can't show what changed — no run history is available yet; enable monitoring/scheduling so the Job can build history over time.

messages.content: Walk me through alice@co's activity over the last 24 hours.

script.text: alice@co's 24-hour timeline includes 6 engine events, such as an interactive query against Sales costing 12.0 CU-seconds.

messages.content: Run this ad-hoc KQL against the capacity engine: CapacityEvents | take 1

script.text: I can't run that — no live query engine is configured yet; enable monitoring to run ad-hoc KQL.

messages.content: what proven capacity query templates can I run?

script.text: There are 21 ready-to-run templates; for capacity, capacity-peak-windows-24h finds the peak CU windows over the last day.

messages.content: why was the capacity throttled yesterday?

script.text: Throttling is ruled out - the throttle gate did not pass: CU stayed within smoothing and no throttle signal fired in the window. High CU alone is not throttling. No capacity-side cause; if reports felt slow, the cause is elsewhere (model or query).

messages.content: who is driving the load right now?

script.text: I can't determine that - the telemetry source returned no data for the window (data unavailable). That makes this branch inconclusive, not healthy: absence of data is not absence of problems. Enable monitoring on the workspace to close this blind spot.

messages.content: is one user causing the spike on Ent-Reporting-Sales?

script.text: No - the differential rules out single-user blame: the top user holds only ~9% of the item's monitored CU (proxy, not billed CU) and the load is spread across hundreds of users. The item itself is expensive for everyone; the fix target is the model, not a person.

messages.content: has this happened before or is it new?

script.text: I can't check recurrence yet - there is no run history available in this environment, so comparing against past runs is impossible. This is inconclusive, not a clean bill: once the scheduled sweep records runs, I can tell you whether this repeats.

messages.content: why do refreshes keep failing and what should we do?

script.text: Root cause: refresh contention - four semantic-model refreshes overlap in the same hour and queue each other out. The fix: stagger the two heaviest schedules out of that window and enable incremental refresh on the largest model; the model owner should make both changes.

messages.content: who had the biggest moments of capacity usage today, with % of base capacity?

script.text: The biggest timepoint peak today was a single MDX query on Ent-Reporting-Sales at 14:09 UTC (10:09 AM EDT) that hit about 1.6% of base capacity for its 30-second timepoint slice. Nothing today crossed into a high per-operation share -- the per-query load on this F1024 stays in the low single-digit percent of base, so the day's pressure is coming from many concurrent operations rather than any one user's query. Want me to check whether those concurrent moments pushed the capacity total over 100%?

messages.content: when did total CU go over 100% today and who contributed?

script.text: The capacity crossed 100% in a sustained block this afternoon, peaking at 141.6% at 20:26 UTC (4:26 PM EDT). That block was almost entirely interactive (background stayed ~9%), and the contributor was Paul.Sugarman -- two long MDX queries on Ent-Reporting-Sales running through the window. Separately, one 30-second window hit an extreme total driven almost entirely by background work, not user queries -- that one is a system/refresh workload to chase, not a user. Want me to pull Sugarman's two queries to see what made them so heavy?
